plan_adherence_notes: All 3 plan steps completed exactly as specified. All 6 test cases from step 1 are present and match the plan description. Step 2 (run new tests) and step 3 (full suite) both verified as passing. Minor deduction: plan step 1 said 'minWidth 0' for probability 0, and the test correctly checks this, but the plan said 'minWidth 2' for probability 0.5 which was also correctly implemented. Execution was faithful.
missed_questions: Should accessibility attributes (aria-label, role) be tested for the bar segments?
Should the component be tested with fractional probabilities that produce rounding edge cases (e.g., 0.333 → 33.3%)?
reason: Execution closely followed the plan. All 6 tests pass and cover the task requirements: probability boundary values (0, 0.5, 1), percentage text display (both title attributes and visible spans), bar width styling (inline width, minWidth, transition), and multi-outcome color cycling. Research was thorough — all 3 questions were well-formed and directly informed plan decisions. The 2 missed questions are minor: accessibility testing is a nice-to-have for a display component, and rounding edge cases are already implicitly covered by the formatProbability utility (tested elsewhere). No regressions in the full suite.
